articles.title: The JavaScript Date Quiz
articles.description: Prepare to get irritated? JavaScript’s native date parsing features are notoriously arcane and prone to cause surprises if you step off the beaten track. So while we await the broad availability of the Temporal API, why not put your assumptions and knowledge to the test with an educational quiz?
articles.author: Sam Rose

articles.title: Next.js 15.4 Released (and What's Coming in Next.js 16)
articles.description: A relatively small release for Next, but with updates to performance, stability, and Turbopack compatibility, and a good summary of what’s coming next in Next.js 16.
articles.author: Jimmy Lai and Zack Tanner

articles.title: WebAssembly: Yes, But for What?
articles.description: Writing for ACM Queue, one of the contributors to multiple JavaScript and WebAssembly (WASM) implementations shares a good roundup of where WebAssembly is being used, both in the browser and server-side, and how it’s gradually finding its way into seemingly everything.
articles.author: Andy Wingo / ACM

articles.title: How to Create an NPM Package in 2025
articles.description: One of JavaScript’s most essential tasks, but one with numerous steps involved if you want to follow best practices, integrate useful tools, and get things just right. Matt Pocock rounds up the overall process here.
articles.author: Matt Pocock

articles.title: The History of React Through Code
articles.description: An epic article charting React’s evolution from its origins at Facebook through to now. It sheds light on React’s core philosophies and the motivations behind major decisions. This is a great way to round out your thinking about, and knowledge of, React's overall story.
articles.author: Corbin Crutchley

articles.title: ▶  The Untold Story of JavaScript
articles.description: Two months ago, the Deno team shared A Brief History of JavaScript, a thorough timeline-based tour of JavaScript each year from 1994 till now. This video covers the same ground in just 8 minutes.
articles.author: Deno

articles.title: A Better Promise.all() – Utility Types and Functions
articles.description: Utility types and functions that make deep promise handling more ergonomic and type-safe.
articles.author: Nick Keuning

articles.title: Tiptap v3: The Headless Rich Text Editor Framework
articles.description: Tiptap provides a fantastic base for putting together powerful rich text editing experiences, and v3 includes a lot of DX improvements like being able to unmount and remount editors (ideal for dynamic UIs), ‘Markviews’ for creating custom views for text segments (marks) using your own components, an SSR mode, and more. GitHub repo.
articles.author: Tiptap GmbH

articles.title: ✉️ Upyo: A Simple Cross-Runtime Email Sending Library
articles.description: A cross-runtime email library that provides a unified, type-safe API for sending emails both on SMTP and HTTP-based (e.g. SendGrid or Amazon SES) providers. TIL that ‘upyo’ (우표) means ‘postage stamp’ in Korean.
articles.author: Hong Minhee

articles.title: Hyper Fetch: A 'Turbocharged' Fetch Library for Working with Remote APIs
articles.description: A framework-agnostic, Axios and TanStack Query-inspired type-safe data-fetching framework for browser and server environments, with request lifecycle management, real-time communication, progress tracking, and codegen for Swagger/OpenAPI. GitHub repo.
articles.author: Maciej Pyrc et al.

articles.title: GrowField: Small, Dependency-Free Module for Making Textarea Elements Grow
articles.description: Very simple. For when you’ve got a textarea input and you want it to grow as more content is added to it.
articles.author: Five Fifteen

articles.description: Vue 3.6 Alpha has been released as a preview of what's coming up. Vapor Mode is a key addition for compiling single file components into a more efficient form.
React Native is gaining support for Node-API, opening up a lot of possibilities for code-sharing, build optimizations, and bringing existing packages into the React Native ecosystem.
The Node.js team is discussing whether to move Node to annual major releases (as opposed to twice a year now).
You can now run Laravel (PHP) apps within Node.js.
Nuxt v4.0 – A major DX-focused release for the popular full-stack Vue.js framework.
Node.js v24.4.1, v22.17.1, and v20.19.4 – To fix security vulnerabilities.
ESLint v9.31.0 – Four core rules have been updated to support explicit resource management.
Astro 5.12, Neutralinojs 6.2, OpenPGP.js 6.2

articles.title: A Friendly Introduction to SVG
articles.description: Josh has plenty of experience with creating fun Scalable Vector Graphics — here, he shares what you need to know by way of a foundational overview, complete with interactive code examples and demos. A superb resource, whether you’re completely new to using SVGs or want a solid refresher. Good stuff.
articles.author: Josh W. Comeau

articles.title: The State of HTML 2025 Survey Is Now Open
articles.description: The third annual State of HTML survey returns to check in on how we’re all using the web platform’s growing list of capabilities. Lea Verou is back at the helm and she’s blogged about things here, noting how the results directly feed into prioritization for next year’s Interop project. 🗳️ Tell em we sent you!
articles.author: Devographics

articles.title: WCAG in Plain English: Making Accessibility Standards Easy to Understand
articles.description: A searchable resource that provides a beginner-friendly guide to the official Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG), to make them a little easier to digest but not to be viewed as a replacement.
articles.author: AAArdvark

articles.title: So Many Ranges, So Little Time: A Cheatsheet of animation-ranges for Your Next Scroll-Driven Animation
articles.description: A solid primer on scroll-driven animations and the various options now at our disposal.
articles.author: Saron Yitbarek

articles.title: ▶  Next-Level Features Every Web Dev Should Know
articles.description: In under ten minutes Jad shares a high-level overview of various modern features worth knowing, including Container queries, scroll-state(), dialog, Document Picture-in-Picture, and more.
articles.author: Jad Joubran

articles.title: Getting Clarity on Apple's Liquid Glass
articles.description: This is a good round-up on all things Liquid Glass so far, including its introduction, the developer reaction, accessibility concerns, plus experiments with its intended style. A solid grab bag of stuff on the topic.
articles.author: Geoff Graham

articles.title: Why I Don’t Trust WCAG 2.2 and What I’m Hoping From 3.0
articles.description: Daniel highlights some of his various concerns with WCAG 2.2 and touches on how WCAG 3.0 should help things.
articles.author: Daniel Schwarz

articles.title: Tiny Screens, Big Impact: The Forgotten Art of Developing Web Apps For Feature Phones
articles.description: Why flip phones still matter in the year 2025, and how we can build and launch web apps for these tiny class of devices.
articles.author: Tom Barrasso

articles.title: Tailwind is the 'Worst of All Worlds'
articles.description: “a regrettable step backwards that takes everything bad about CSS and modern web development and brings it all together in one library”
articles.author: Colton Voege opinion

articles.title: Frontend Performance Checklist for 2025
articles.description: A high-level laundry list of things to keep in mind when trying to create fast, efficient web apps.
articles.author: Hegna, Dwivedi, Radakovic

articles.title: Glass3D Generator: A Tool to Generate a 3D Glass Effect with CSS
articles.description: You can edit backdrop-filter settings, color, and texture – all previewed live on the page. You can also test three different background types under the glass effect (dark, light, and video). Handy maybe in the upcoming era of Liquid Glass?
articles.author: Glass3D Generator

articles.title: Visual CSS: Edit Any Website's CSS Visually
articles.description: Enter a URL and this tool will display the page with various CSS editing tools, including measuring, wireframing, responsive testing, and others. There’s also an AI prompt for describing changes you want to make.
articles.author: Visual CSS

articles.title: Astro 5.12 Released
articles.description: Features an upgraded Netlify dev experience, TOML support in content loaders, and more.
articles.author: Matt Kane

articles.title: Tiptap v3: The Headless Rich Text Editor Framework
articles.description: Provides a fantastic base for putting together powerful rich text editing experiences. v3 includes a lot of DX improvements like being able to unmount and remount editors (ideal for dynamic UIs), ‘Markviews’ for creating custom views for text segments (marks) using your own components, an SSR mode, and more. GitHub repo.
articles.author: Tiptap GmbH

articles.title: GrowField: Small, Dependency-Free Module for Making Textarea Elements Grow
articles.description: Very simple. For when you’ve got a textarea input and you want it to grow as more content is added to it.
articles.author: Five Fifteen
